edition.dagbeeld: De uitvaart van Khamenei domineert het internationale nieuws, terwijl Iran de Straat van Hormuz verder op slot gooit en de opvolgingskwestie onopgelost blijft. Op de achtergrond tekent zich een bredere verschuiving af: Europa wapent zich, de NAVO heroriënteert zich, en Trumps Amerika viert zijn 250ste verjaardag met een toespraak die meer leek op een campagnerally.

article.title: Iran seeks to tighten control over strait of Hormuz alongside Khamenei funeral
article.summary: IRGC warnings force ships in strait to turn back as Tehran uses passages from Qur’an to send messages to Gulf delegations

opening.note: Iran grijpt de begrafenis van Khamenei aan om de Straat van Hormuz te vergrendelen — geopolitiek hete provocatie.
opening.label: breaking

article.title: Saving Nato in the era of Trump
article.summary: The big questions hanging over the alliance will not be discussed in Ankara

front.note: FT doorprikt wat er in Ankara echt niet besproken wordt over NATOs toekomst.
front.label: analyse

article.title: ‘The risk is Russia becomes desperate’: the Swedish Baltic Sea island preparing for invasion
article.summary: Civilian resilience initiatives and young military conscripts are being readied should Putin hope to test Nato’s resolve

front.note: Op een Zweeds Oostzee-eiland bereidt men zich voor op een Russische invasie.
front.label: reportage

article.title: Peruvian and Colombian presidential elections: Expatriate vote helps far right clinch victory
article.summary: Peruvians living abroad helped Keiko Fujimori to win the presidency, even though only a minority of Peru's residents voted for her. Expatriate Colombians also massively backed Abelardo de la Espriella, though this was not as decisive.

front.note: Diaspora-stemmen tillen uiterst rechts naar de macht in Peru én Colombia.
front.label: analyse

article.title: What Trump’s July 4 Speech Revealed
article.summary: The president demanded credit for a history that he regards as a sucker’s mistake.

article.title: Parliament watchdog urged to investigate Nigel Farage over benefits from fraudster
article.summary: Call for new inquiry into ‘in-kind’ benefits from aide before Reform leader’s election as MP in July 2024

front.note: Parlementaire waakhond gevraagd Farage opnieuw te onderzoeken om fraudeur-voordelen.
front.label: onderzoek

article.title: Chemical accidents rise as Trump administration proposes weakening safety rules
article.summary: Chemicals from accidents that injured or killed people increased by nearly 50 percent in recent years.

front.note: Chemische ongelukken stegen met 50% terwijl Trump de veiligheidsregels verder afbouwt.
front.label: onderzoek

article.title: Heatwave: Fires devastate forests across Europe
article.summary: Hundreds of firefighters battled infernos that burnt thousands of hectares in France, Spain and Portugal, three countries where, in some places, temperatures were predicted to reach 40ºC on Sunday.

front.note: Bosbranden teisteren Frankrijk, Spanje en Portugal tijdens extreme hittegolf.
front.label: reportage

items.title: Chinese underground church figure Jin Mingri freed from prison
items.summary: The Zion Church founder's release comes after a direct plea to Xi Jinping from Donald Trump.

items.title: Turkey sees its status in NATO rise, thanks to its weapons factories and Trump
items.summary: A friendship between presidents Donald Trump and Recep Tayyip Erdogan, and a need for weapons production, has changed how other NATO countries treat Turkey.

items.title: Oekraïne treft weer grote Russische olieterminal, Trump belt met Poetin en Zelensky
Topgeneraal Eichelsheim: ‘We hebben afgesproken dat we intensiever gaan samenwerken met Turkije’
Nigeria says two nationals killed in South Africa amid rise of anti-migrant attacks
items.summary: Nigeria's government said one of its nationals was reportedly killed by police officers "using gruesome interrogation techniques".

items.title: OPEC Plus Pledges to Pump More Even as Oil Prices Fall
items.summary: The cartel’s decision comes as U.S.-Iran talks to end the war have eased pressure on oil supplies, even as broader market and political volatility persists.

items.title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
items.summary: "Group project, but make it 1776." That's how a new commercial for Google Workspace opens. And things only get cringier from there. The clip imagines what it would be like if the founding fathers turned to Google's collaboration tools and Gemini to help them draft the Declaration of Independence. Ben Franklin texts Tho

items.title: Philosophers Are the Latest Hiring Target for AI Companies
items.summary: A.I. labs are hiring contrarian, chin-stroking, finger-steepling sages. Who’s underemployed now?

items.title: OpenAI’s apparent failure to visit key site raises questions over UK investment
items.summary: Exclusive: £20bn of ‘potential’ £30bn AI investment touted by UK ministers appears to have been hypothetical

items.title: In San Francisco’s A.I. Era, Even $180,000 Tech Salaries Are No Longer Enough
items.summary: As OpenAI and Anthropic prepare to go public, tech workers making six figures are grousing that they cannot compete with the new A.I. elite. Some doubt they can afford to stay.

items.title: Australia probes mystery space balls that washed up on beach
items.summary: Officials are searching for the origins of six pieces of space debris discovered on Forrest Beach in Queensland.

items.title: The Sourdough Sidekick automates the boring bit of baking
items.summary: Baking sourdough bread is inherently old-fashioned, relying on natural fermentation and wild yeast instead of the simple, predictable commercial stuff. So it might sound anathema to bring a gadget into the mix. The trick to the Sourdough Sidekick - backed and branded by King Arthur flour - is that it promises to automa

items.title: Gaza protests against two Democrats spark outcry and debate on tactics
items.summary: Incidents involving California state senator Scott Wiener and New York congressman Dan Goldman underscore Israel-Palestine conflict’s role in US elections

items.title: Families of children killed in bombing of Iranian school join leader’s funeral
items.summary: In Iran, the school deaths have become a symbol for U.S.-Israeli brutality and an unjust war. The Washington Post is reporting from the country under restricted conditions.

items.title: Clavicular: An American masculinist in Paris gets rejected
items.summary: The young influencer rose to fame on social media with his hyper-masculine and aggressive videos. Today, he is widely mocked for his unsuccessful attempts at flirting, which he broadcast live from the streets of Paris.

items.title: Delivery firm Evri sues BBC for £1.2m over Panorama documentary
items.summary: Company seeks redress for contracts it says it has lost as a result of programme’s claims about its business practices

items.title: Trump’s $2bn bonanza heralds the rise of political grifters across the west
items.summary: With voters embracing leaders who brazenly monetise public office, experts say an ethical code is breaking down

items.title: Beyoncé releases surprise new track for Fourth of July weekend
items.summary: Morning Dew (Donk) reportedly conceived for Beyoncé’s second album B’Day, which will be reissued for its 20th anniversary in September

items.title: De Lelystadse jeugdzorg wordt steeds duurder. De directeur die daar iets aan deed, moest vertrekken
items.summary: In Lelystad woedde het afgelopen halfjaar een ongekende politieke machtsstrijd rondom jeugdhulp. Een experiment om de stijgende kosten in de hand te houden, kwam tot een lelijk einde. Daarmee verdoezelt de gemeente hoe slecht zij zelf heeft gepresteerd.

items.title: Pioneer of ‘extreme male brain’ theory of autism now says phrase unhelpful
items.summary: Exclusive: Prof Simon Baron-Cohen says his language was misunderstood and it is a myth that autistic people lack empathy

items.title: Trump Administration Rolls Back Dozens of Gun Regulations
items.summary: Critics say the administration is weakening public safety. Proponents say regulations would be where they were before President Joseph R. Biden took office.

items.title: To Beat the Heat, the Ultrarich Have Snow Rooms
items.summary: Indoors, for a fortunate few, it’s always a winter wonderland.

items.title: The Brooklyn Bridge Briefly Catches Fire During a Fireworks Display
items.summary: The fire, ignited by Fourth of July fireworks, was extinguished by shortly after 10 p.m., the police said. No injuries were reported.

items.title: In San Francisco’s A.I. Era, Even $180,000 Tech Salaries Are No Longer Enough
items.summary: As OpenAI and Anthropic prepare to go public, tech workers making six figures are grousing that they cannot compete with the new A.I. elite. Some doubt they can afford to stay.

items.title: Nigel Farage did not declare gifts from crypto entrepreneur convicted of fraud
items.summary: Reform UK’s Robert Jenrick says Farage accepted staff, security and accommodation from George Cottrell before becoming an MP

items.title: Majority of UK smokers wrongly believe vaping is as harmful as cigarettes, experts find
items.summary: Analysis shows public understanding about vaping, which is far less harmful than smoking, has plummeted in past decade

items.title: Pioneer of ‘extreme male brain’ theory of autism now says phrase unhelpful
items.summary: Exclusive: Prof Simon Baron-Cohen says his language was misunderstood and it is a myth that autistic people lack empathy

items.title: Marjan Berk had een monter soort ironie, als actrice én schrijfster
Een spic en span Turkije ontvangt de NAVO-top, maar achter de schermen ziet het er minder mooi uit
items.summary: Komende week komen 32 leiders in Ankara bijeen voor de jaarlijkse NAVO-top. Gastheer is de Turkse president Erdogan. Jarenlang gold Turkije onder zijn leiding als het lastige buitenbeentje, maar nu kan de alliantie nauwelijks meer om hem heen.

items.title: Jong, rechts en op ramkoers met de asielminister: in Oldebroek en Maassluis wordt de spreidingswet actief tegengewerkt
items.summary: Lokale rechts-radicale verkiezingswinnaars Tom de Nooijer (Oldebroek) en Gary Bouwer (Maassluis) sorteren voor op een politieke confrontatie met de landelijke overheid, over de taakstelling voor op te vangen asielzoekers. „Mijn handtekening staat er niet onder.”
